module.comment: Module system. `use`/`open` are true keywords; `raw` (only after `use`) and `given` (only after `open`) are identifiers the parser recognizes structurally, so they are highlighted positionally, never as bare words.

declarations.comment: Declarations are requests (COMPILATION.typ § Declarations as requests): `head? NAME (: T)? (:= v)?`. The heads — let / sig / base / given and guard <policy> — are LIBRARY VALUES (cut.disp, std/relation.disp), not keywords. They are highlighted only in head position (member start followed by a declared name and ':'/':='), mirroring the parser's isDeclStart; mid-expression uses stay plain identifiers. Custom decorator heads exist but are not in this closed set, so they stay plain too.

patterns.comment: guard <policy atoms…> NAME — decorator head atoms are line-local (parser guarantee), so one line-rule is faithful. The gap excludes ':' (a top-level colon ends a head) plus comment/string starts, so `guard : T := v` — the definition of guard itself — falls through to the plain-declaration rule.
let / sig / base / given NAME — covers the top-level decorators and the braced lexical `let`

conventional `test` prefix on an equation item `lhs = rhs` (the prelude identity, peeled by the elaborator) — member-start only; `test : T := v` (the prelude's definition of test itself) is a plain declaration, excluded by the lookahead. The optional trailing capture colors the equation's applied head like any call site.
plain declaration `NAME := v` / `NAME : T := v` — highlight the bound name
member position inside braces: record fields, `use "f" { x := v }` fills, `open given { X : T }` entries, named args

binders.comment: Lambda binder `{a, b, c} -> body` — a comma list of plain names directly before an arrow. Names get parameter scope (the Rust-like light blue); annotated braces (`{A : Type}`) contain ':' so they fall through to the member/type rules.

applications.comment: Function-position identifiers — the Rust-like call-site yellow. In disp application is juxtaposition, so the head of an application spine is an identifier that STARTS an expression (line start, after an opening bracket, separator, ':='/':'/'='/arrows, or if/then/else) AND is applied to at least one atom. The exclusion list keeps keywords, decorator heads, literals, and the leaf out of head position; the starter prefix is re-scoped by the included rules so consumed operators/keywords keep their colors.

guard-policies.comment: std/relation guard policies — colored only inside a guard head (included from the declarations rule), plain values elsewhere
guard-policies.name: storage.modifier.policy.disp

types.comment: Capitalized identifiers: type formers and coproduct constructors
types.name: entity.name.type.disp

identifiers.comment: Everything else lowercase = variables/arguments, the Rust-like light-blue body color; the applications rule has already claimed the heads
identifiers.name: variable.other.disp
